86#define NATS_DEFAULT_URL "nats://localhost:4222"
87
97 #define JSMsgSize "Nats-Msg-Size"
98
111 #define JSMsgRollup "Nats-Rollup"
112
120 #define JSMsgRollupSubject "sub"
121
129 #define JSMsgRollupAll "all"
130
131 // Headers for republished messages and direct get.
132 #define JSStream "Nats-Stream"
133 #define JSSequence "Nats-Sequence"
134 #define JSLastSequence "Nats-Last-Sequence"
135 #define JSTimeStamp "Nats-Time-Stamp"
136 #define JSSubject "Nats-Subject"
137

139// Types.

153
159typedef struct __natsStatistics natsStatistics;
160

197typedef void (*natsThreadStartedHandler)(void *closure);
198#endif
199
205typedef struct __natsClientConfig
206{
207 int64_t DefaultWriteDeadline;
208
209 int64_t LockSpinCount;
210
211 // Subscription message delivery thread control
212 bool DefaultToThreadPool;
213 int ThreadPoolMax;
214
215 // Reply message delivery thread control
216 bool DefaultRepliesToThreadPool;
217 bool UseSeparatePoolForReplies;
218 int ReplyThreadPoolMax;
219
220 // Callback invoked when a thread created by the library starts
221 natsThreadStartedHandler ThreadStartedHandler;
222 void *ThreadStartedHandlerClosure;
223
224} natsClientConfig;
225

269 int Count;

324typedef struct jsPubOptions

335 jsScheduleOptions Schedule;
337
338} jsPubOptions;

343typedef enum
344{
345 js_LimitsPolicy = 0,
346 js_InterestPolicy,
347 js_WorkQueuePolicy,
348
349} jsRetentionPolicy;

354typedef enum
355{
356 js_DiscardOld = 0,
357 js_DiscardNew,
358
359} jsDiscardPolicy;

364typedef enum
365{
366 js_FileStorage = 0,
367 js_MemoryStorage,
368
369} jsStorageType;

374typedef enum
375{
376 js_StorageCompressionNone = 0,
377 js_StorageCompressionS2,

383typedef enum
384{
385 js_DeliverAll = 0,
386 js_DeliverLast,
387 js_DeliverNew,
388 js_DeliverByStartSequence,

390 js_DeliverLastPerSubject,
391
392} jsDeliverPolicy;

397typedef enum
398{
399 js_AckExplicit = 0,
400 js_AckNone,
401 js_AckAll,
402
403
404} jsAckPolicy;

409typedef enum
410{
411 js_ReplayInstant = 0,
412 js_ReplayOriginal,

414} jsReplayPolicy;

419typedef enum
420{
421 js_PersistDefault = 0,
422 js_PersistAsync,

436typedef struct jsPlacement

442} jsPlacement;

453typedef struct jsExternalStream
454{
455 const char *APIPrefix;
456 const char *DeliverPrefix;
457
458} jsExternalStream;

472typedef struct jsStreamSource
473{
474 const char *Name;
475 uint64_t OptStartSeq;
476 int64_t OptStartTime;
477 const char *FilterSubject;
478 jsExternalStream *External;
479 // Domain and External are mutually exclusive.
480 // If Domain is set, an External value will be created with
481 // the APIPrefix constructed based on the Domain value.
482 const char *Domain;
483
484} jsStreamSource;
485
489typedef struct jsRePublish
490{
491 const char *Source;
492 const char *Destination;
493 bool HeadersOnly;
494
495} jsRePublish;
496
501typedef struct jsSubjectTransformConfig
502{
503 const char *Source;
504 const char *Destination;
505} jsSubjectTransformConfig;
506
511typedef struct jsStreamConsumerLimits
512{
513 int64_t InactiveThreshold;
514 int MaxAckPending;
515} jsStreamConsumerLimits;
516
596typedef struct jsStreamConfig {
597 const char *Name;
598 const char *Description;
599 const char **Subjects;
600 int SubjectsLen;
601 jsRetentionPolicy Retention;
602 int64_t MaxConsumers;
603 int64_t MaxMsgs;
604 int64_t MaxBytes;
605 int64_t MaxAge;
606 int64_t MaxMsgsPerSubject;
607 int32_t MaxMsgSize;
608 jsDiscardPolicy Discard;
609 jsStorageType Storage;
610 int64_t Replicas;
611 bool NoAck;
612 const char *Template;
613 int64_t Duplicates;
614 jsPlacement *Placement;
615 jsStreamSource *Mirror;
616 jsStreamSource **Sources;
617 int SourcesLen;
618 bool Sealed;
619 bool DenyDelete;
620 bool DenyPurge;
621
624 bool AllowRollup;
625
628 jsRePublish *RePublish;
629
632 bool AllowDirect;
633
636 bool MirrorDirect;
637
640 bool DiscardNewPerSubject;
641
645 natsMetadata Metadata;
646
649 jsStorageCompression Compression;
650
652 uint64_t FirstSeq;
653
656 jsSubjectTransformConfig SubjectTransform;
657
660 jsStreamConsumerLimits ConsumerLimits;
661
664 bool AllowMsgTTL;
665
669 int64_t SubjectDeleteMarkerTTL;
670
673 jsPersistModeType PersistMode;
674
676 bool AllowAtomic;
677
680 bool AllowMsgCounter;
681
684 bool AllowMsgSchedules;
685
688 bool AllowBatched;
689
690} jsStreamConfig;
691

696{
697 uint64_t *Msgs;

699 uint64_t Bytes;
700
701} jsLostStreamData;

748

757typedef struct jsStreamState
758{
759 uint64_t Msgs;
760 uint64_t Bytes;
761 uint64_t FirstSeq;
762 int64_t FirstTime;
763 uint64_t LastSeq;
764 int64_t LastTime;
765 int64_t NumSubjects;
766 jsStreamStateSubjects *Subjects;
767 uint64_t NumDeleted;
768 uint64_t *Deleted;
769 int DeletedLen;
770 jsLostStreamData *Lost;
771 int64_t Consumers;
772
773} jsStreamState;

779typedef struct jsPeerInfo
780{

783 bool Offline;
784 int64_t Active;
785 uint64_t Lag;

787} jsPeerInfo;

794{

803
804} jsClusterInfo;

818
819} jsStreamSourceInfo;

827 const char *Domain;
828 const char *Cluster;
829
830} jsStreamAlternate;

838typedef struct jsStreamInfo
839{

874{
875 char **List;

923typedef struct jsConsumerConfig
924{
925 const char *Name;
926 const char *Durable;
927 const char *Description;
928 jsDeliverPolicy DeliverPolicy;
929 uint64_t OptStartSeq;
930 int64_t OptStartTime;
931 jsAckPolicy AckPolicy;
932 int64_t AckWait;
933 int64_t MaxDeliver;
934 int64_t *BackOff;
935 int BackOffLen;
936 const char *FilterSubject;
937 jsReplayPolicy ReplayPolicy;
938 uint64_t RateLimit;
939 const char *SampleFrequency;
940 int64_t MaxWaiting;
941 int64_t MaxAckPending;
942 bool FlowControl;
943 int64_t Heartbeat;
944 bool HeadersOnly;
945
946 // Pull based options.
947 int64_t MaxRequestBatch;
948 int64_t MaxRequestExpires;
949 int64_t MaxRequestMaxBytes;
950
951 // Push based options.
952 const char *DeliverSubject;
953 const char *DeliverGroup;
954
955 // Ephemeral inactivity threshold.
956 int64_t InactiveThreshold;
957
958 // Generally inherited by parent stream and other markers, now can be configured directly.
959 int64_t Replicas;
960 // Force memory storage.
961 bool MemoryStorage;
962
963 // Configuration options introduced in 2.10
964
965 const char **FilterSubjects;
966 int FilterSubjectsLen;
967 natsMetadata Metadata;
968
969 // Configuration options introduced in 2.11
970
974 int64_t PauseUntil;
975
979 const char *PriorityPolicy;
980
985 int64_t PinnedTTL;
986
988 const char **PriorityGroups;
989 int PriorityGroupsLen;
990} jsConsumerConfig;
991
1009typedef struct jsConsumerSequenceMismatch
1010{
1011 uint64_t Stream;
1012 uint64_t ConsumerClient;
1013 uint64_t ConsumerServer;
1014
1015} jsConsumerSequenceMismatch;

1281typedef struct jsPubAck
1282{

1284 uint64_t Sequence;

1286 bool Duplicate;
1287 char *Batch;
1288 uint64_t Count;
1289 char *Value;
1290
1291} jsPubAck;

1297typedef struct jsPubAckErr
1298{
1299 natsMsg *Msg;
1300 natsStatus Err;
1301 jsErrCode ErrCode;
1302 const char *ErrText;
1303
1304} jsPubAckErr;

1307// Forward declarations
1308typedef void (*jsPubAckErrHandler)(jsCtx *js, jsPubAckErr *pae, void *closure);
1309typedef void (*jsPubAckHandler)(jsCtx *js, natsMsg *msg, jsPubAck *pa, jsPubAckErr *pae, void *closure);
1310#endif

1321typedef struct jsDirectGetMsgOptions
1322{
1323 uint64_t Sequence;
1324 const char *NextBySubject;
1325 const char *LastBySubject;
1326
1327} jsDirectGetMsgOptions;

1334typedef struct jsFetchRequest
1335{
1336 int64_t Expires;
1337 int Batch;
1338 int64_t MaxBytes;
1339 bool NoWait;
1340 int64_t Heartbeat;
1341
1342 // The use of these fields require nats-server v2.11.0 or later
1343 int64_t MinPending;
1344 int64_t MinAckPending;
1345 const char *ID;
1347 const char *Group;
1348 // The use of this field require nats-server v2.12.0 or later
1349 uint8_t Priority;
1352
1353} jsFetchRequest;
1354
1376typedef void (*jsFetchCompleteHandler)(natsConnection *nc, natsSubscription *sub, natsStatus s, void *closure);
1377
1388typedef bool (*jsFetchNextHandler)(int *messages, int64_t *maxBytes, natsSubscription *sub, void *closure);
1389
1395typedef struct jsOptionsPullSubscribeAsync
1396{
1397 int64_t Timeout;
1398 int MaxMessages;
1399 int64_t MaxBytes;
1400
1408 bool NoWait;
1409
1413 jsFetchCompleteHandler CompleteHandler;
1414 void *CompleteHandlerClosure;
1415
1418 int64_t Heartbeat;
1419
1424 const char *Group;
1425
1431 int64_t MinPending;
1432
1438 int64_t MinAckPending;
1439
1443 int FetchSize;
1444
1451 int KeepAhead;
1452
1458 jsFetchNextHandler NextHandler;
1459 void *NextHandlerClosure;
1460
1461} jsOptionsPullSubscribeAsync;
1462
1468typedef struct jsOptionsPublishAsync
1469{
1470 int64_t MaxPending;
1471
1472 // If jsPubAckHandler is specified, the callback will be invoked
1473 // for every asynchronous published message, either as a positive
1474 // result, or with the error encountered when publishing that
1475 // message. If this callback is specified, ErrHandler (see below)
1476 // will be ignored.
1477 jsPubAckHandler AckHandler;
1478 void *AckHandlerClosure;
1479
1480 // This callback is invoked for messages published asynchronously
1481 // when an error is returned by the server or if the library has
1482 // timed-out waiting for an acknowledgment back from the server
1483 // (if publish uses the jsPubOptions.MaxWait).
1484 jsPubAckErrHandler ErrHandler;
1485 void *ErrHandlerClosure;
1486
1487 int64_t StallWait;
1488
1489 bool MuxReplies;
1493
1494} jsOptionsPublishAsync;
1495
1505typedef struct jsOptionsStreamPurge
1506{
1507 const char *Subject;
1508 uint64_t Sequence;
1509 uint64_t Keep;
1510
1511} jsOptionsStreamPurge;

1516typedef struct jsOptionsStreamInfo
1517{
1518 bool DeletedDetails;
1519 const char *SubjectsFilter;
1520
1521} jsOptionsStreamInfo;
1522
1529typedef struct jsOptionsStream
1530{
1531 jsOptionsStreamPurge Purge;
1532 jsOptionsStreamInfo Info;
1533
1534} jsOptionsStream;

1541typedef struct jsOptions
1542{
1543 const char *Prefix;
1544 const char *Domain;
1545 int64_t Wait;
1546 jsOptionsPublishAsync PublishAsync;
1547 jsOptionsPullSubscribeAsync PullSubscribeAsync;
1548 jsOptionsStream Stream;
1549
1550} jsOptions;

1555typedef struct __kvStore kvStore;
1556
1560typedef struct __kvEntry kvEntry;
1561
1565typedef struct __kvStatus kvStatus;
1566
1570typedef struct __kvWatcher kvWatcher;
1571
1575typedef enum
1576{
1577 kvOp_Unknown = 0,
1578 kvOp_Put,
1579 kvOp_Delete,
1580 kvOp_Purge,
1581
1582} kvOperation;
1583
1589typedef struct kvConfig
1590{
1591 const char *Bucket;
1592 const char *Description;
1593 int32_t MaxValueSize;
1594 uint8_t History;
1595 int64_t TTL;
1596 int64_t MaxBytes;
1597 jsStorageType StorageType;
1598 int Replicas;
1599 jsRePublish *RePublish;
1600 jsStreamSource *Mirror;
1601 jsStreamSource **Sources;
1602 int SourcesLen;
1603
1604 // When > 0 (nanoseconds), enables per-message TTL on the bucket's
1605 // backing stream and auto-expires the
1606 // delete/purge tombstone markers after this duration. Required for
1607 // kvStore_CreateWithTTL() to take effect. Requires nats-server v2.11+.
1608 int64_t LimitMarkerTTL;
1609
1610} kvConfig;
1611
1617typedef struct kvWatchOptions
1618{
1623 bool IgnoreDeletes;
1624
1631 bool IncludeHistory;
1632
1637 bool MetaOnly;
1638
1643 int64_t Timeout;
1644
1654 bool UpdatesOnly;
1655
1662 int64_t Heartbeat;
1663
1674 uint64_t ResumeFromRevision;
1675
1676} kvWatchOptions;
1677
1683typedef struct kvPurgeOptions
1684{
1685 // How long to wait (in milliseconds) for some operations to complete.
1686 int64_t Timeout;
1687
1688 // When calling kvStore_PurgeDeletes(), all keys that have a delete or
1689 // purge marker as the last entry are gathered and then those keys
1690 // are purged of their content, including the marker.
1691 // Starting with NATS C client v3.3.0, if this option is not specified,
1692 // only the markers older than 30 minutes will be deleted. Use this
1693 // option to set the limit or a negative value to force removal of
1694 // markers regardless of their age.
1695 // The value is expressed as a time in nanoseconds.
1696 int64_t DeleteMarkersOlderThan;
1697
1698 // When > 0, the purge marker
1699 // written by kvStore_Purge() is published with this per-message TTL (in
1700 // milliseconds), so the server auto-expires the purge tombstone after the
1701 // duration. Requires the bucket created with kvConfig.LimitMarkerTTL > 0 and
1702 // nats-server v2.11+. Ignored by kvStore_Delete() (delete markers use the
1703 // bucket-level LimitMarkerTTL).
1704 int64_t TTL;
1705
1706} kvPurgeOptions;
1707
1725typedef struct kvEntryList
1726{
1727 kvEntry **Entries;
1728 int Count;
1729
1730} kvEntryList;

1750{

1753
1754} kvKeysList;

2056
2057} objStoreMeta;
2058
2062typedef struct objStoreInfo
2063{
2067 objStoreMeta Meta;
2068
2072 const char *Bucket;
2073
2077 const char *NUID;
2078
2082 uint64_t Size;
2083
2087 int64_t ModTime;
2088
2094 uint32_t Chunks;
2095
2101 const char *Digest;
2102
2106 bool Deleted;
2107
2108} objStoreInfo;
2109
2117typedef struct objStoreInfoList
2118{
2119 objStoreInfo **List;
2120 int Count;
2121
2122} objStoreInfoList;
2123
2135typedef struct objStoreOptions
2136{
2143 bool ShowDeleted;
2144
2145} objStoreOptions;
2146
2152typedef struct objStoreWatchOptions
2153{
2160 bool IgnoreDeletes;
2161
2168 bool UpdatesOnly;
2169
2170} objStoreWatchOptions;
2171
2172
2173#if defined(NATS_HAS_STREAMING)
2178typedef struct __stanConnection stanConnection;
2179
2184typedef struct __stanSubscription stanSubscription;
2185
2190typedef struct __stanMsg stanMsg;
2191
2196typedef struct __stanConnOptions stanConnOptions;
2197
2202typedef struct __stanSubOptions stanSubOptions;
2203#endif
2204
// end of typesGroup
2206
2207//
2208// Callbacks.
2209//
2210
2233typedef void (*natsMsgHandler)(
2234 natsConnection *nc, natsSubscription *sub, natsMsg *msg, void *closure);
2235
2249typedef void (*natsConnectionHandler)(
2250 natsConnection *nc, void *closure);
2251
2263typedef natsStatus (*natsProxyConnHandler)(
2264 natsSock *fd, char *host, int port, void *closure);
2265
2272typedef void (*natsErrHandler)(
2273 natsConnection *nc, natsSubscription *subscription, natsStatus err,
2274 void *closure);
2275
2289typedef natsStatus (*natsEvLoop_Attach)(
2290 void **userData,
2291 void *loop,
2292 natsConnection *nc,
2293 natsSock socket);
2294
2304typedef natsStatus (*natsEvLoop_ReadAddRemove)(
2305 void *userData,
2306 bool add);
2307
2317typedef natsStatus (*natsEvLoop_WriteAddRemove)(
2318 void *userData,
2319 bool add);
2320
2328typedef natsStatus (*natsEvLoop_Detach)(
2329 void *userData);
2330
2352typedef natsStatus (*natsUserJWTHandler)(
2353 char **userJWT,
2354 char **customErrTxt,
2355 void *closure);
2356
2357
2383typedef natsStatus (*natsSignatureHandler)(
2384 char **customErrTxt,
2385 unsigned char **signature,
2386 int *signatureLength,
2387 const char *nonce,
2388 void *closure);
2389
2398typedef const char* (*natsTokenHandler)(void *closure);
2399
2400
2408typedef void (*natsOnCompleteCB)(void *closure);
2409
2430typedef int64_t (*natsCustomReconnectDelayHandler)(natsConnection *nc, int attempts, void *closure);
2431
2455typedef int (*natsSSLVerifyCb)(int preverifyOk, void *ctx);
2456
2457#ifdef BUILD_IN_DOXYGEN
2491typedef void (*jsPubAckErrHandler)(jsCtx *js, jsPubAckErr *pae, void *closure);
2492
2534typedef void (*jsPubAckHandler)(jsCtx *js, natsMsg *msg, jsPubAck *pa, jsPubAckErr *pae, void *closure);
2535
2576typedef void (*natsThreadStartedHandler)(void *closure);
2577#endif
2578
2579#if defined(NATS_HAS_STREAMING)
2588typedef void (*stanPubAckHandler)(const char *guid, const char *error, void *closure);
2589
2599typedef void (*stanMsgHandler)(
2600 stanConnection *sc, stanSubscription *sub, const char *channel, stanMsg *msg, void *closure);
2601
2608typedef void (*stanConnectionLostHandler)(
2609 stanConnection *sc, const char* errorTxt, void *closure);
2610#endif
2611
// end of callbacksGroup
2613
2614//
2615// Functions.
2616//

2644nats_OpenWithConfig(natsClientConfig *config);

2660nats_Open(int64_t lockSpinCount);

2668nats_GetVersion(void);

2678nats_GetVersionNumber(void);

2695NATS_EXTERN bool
2696nats_CheckCompatibility(void);
2697#else
2698
2699#define nats_CheckCompatibility() nats_CheckCompatibilityImpl(NATS_VERSION_REQUIRED_NUMBER, \
2700 NATS_VERSION_NUMBER, \
2701 NATS_VERSION_STRING)
2702
2703NATS_EXTERN bool
2704nats_CheckCompatibilityImpl(uint32_t reqVerNumber, uint32_t verNumber, const char *verString);
2705
2706#endif

3039NATS_EXTERN natsStatus
3040natsOptions_Create(natsOptions **newOpts);
3041
3063/*
3064 * The above is for doxygen. The proper syntax for username/password
3065 * is without the '\' character:
3066 *
3067 * nats://localhost:4222
3068 * nats://user@localhost:4222
3069 * nats://user:password@localhost:4222
3070 */

9925//
9926// Microservices.
9927//
9928
9971typedef struct micro_client_s microClient;
9972
9976typedef struct __for_forward_compatibility_only microClientConfig;
9977
9987typedef struct micro_endpoint_s microEndpoint;
9988
9996typedef struct micro_endpoint_config_s microEndpointConfig;
9997
10007typedef struct micro_endpoint_info_s microEndpointInfo;
10008
10017typedef struct micro_endpoint_stats_s microEndpointStats;
10018
10031typedef struct micro_error_s microError;
10032
10040typedef struct micro_group_s microGroup;
10041
10048typedef struct micro_group_config_s microGroupConfig;
10049
10055typedef struct micro_request_s microRequest;
10056
10068typedef struct micro_service_s microService;
10069
10079typedef struct micro_service_config_s microServiceConfig;
10080
10090typedef struct micro_service_info_s microServiceInfo;
10091
10098typedef struct micro_service_stats_s microServiceStats;
10099
10100
10101extern NATS_EXTERN microError *micro_ErrorOutOfMemory;
10102extern NATS_EXTERN microError *micro_ErrorInvalidArg;
10103
// end of microTypes
10105
10124typedef microError *(*microRequestHandler)(microRequest *req);
10125
10148typedef void (*microErrorHandler)(microService *m, microEndpoint *ep, natsStatus s);
10149
10161typedef void (*microDoneHandler)(microService *m);
10162
// end of microCallbacks
10164
10175struct micro_endpoint_config_s
10176{
10182 const char *Name;
10183
10193 const char *Subject;
10194
10199 const char *QueueGroup;
10200
10205 bool NoQueueGroup;
10206
10211 natsMetadata Metadata;
10212
10216 microRequestHandler Handler;
10217
10222 void *State;
10223};
10224
10228struct micro_endpoint_info_s
10229{
10233 const char *Name;
10234
10238 const char *Subject;
10239
10244 const char *QueueGroup;
10245
10250 natsMetadata Metadata;
10251};
10252
10256struct micro_endpoint_stats_s
10257{
10258 const char *Name;
10259 const char *Subject;
10260
10265 const char *QueueGroup;
10266
10270 int64_t NumRequests;
10271
10276 int64_t NumErrors;
10277
10281 int64_t ProcessingTimeSeconds;
10282
10286 int64_t ProcessingTimeNanoseconds;
10287
10291 int64_t AverageProcessingTimeNanoseconds;
10292
10296 char LastErrorString[2048];
10297};
10298
10302struct micro_group_config_s
10303{
10305 const char *Prefix;
10306
10308 const char *QueueGroup;
10309
10311 bool NoQueueGroup;
10312};
10313
10320struct micro_service_config_s
10321{
10327 const char *Name;
10328
10332 const char *Version;
10333
10337 const char *Description;
10338
10343 const char *QueueGroup;
10344
10349 bool NoQueueGroup;
10350
10355 natsMetadata Metadata;
10356
10364 microEndpointConfig *Endpoint;
10365
10373 microRequestHandler StatsHandler;
10374
10382 microErrorHandler ErrHandler;
10383
10392 microDoneHandler DoneHandler;
10393
10401 void *State;
10402};
10403
10409struct micro_service_info_s
10410{
10414 const char *Type;
10415
10419 const char *Name;
10420
10424 const char *Version;
10425
10429 const char *Description;
10430
10434 const char *Id;
10435
10439 natsMetadata Metadata;
10440
10444 microEndpointInfo *Endpoints;
10445
10449 int EndpointsLen;
10450};
10451
10455struct micro_service_stats_s
10456{
10460 const char *Type;
10461
10465 const char *Name;
10466
10470 const char *Version;
10471
10475 const char *Id;
10476
10480 int64_t Started;
10481
10485 microEndpointStats *Endpoints;
10486
10490 int EndpointsLen;
10491};
10492
// end of microStructs
10494
10506#define MICRO_API_PREFIX "$SRV"
10507

10543

10549

10555
// end of microConstants

natsConnection_Buffered

Returns the number of bytes to be sent to the server.
natsConnection_ReadLastError
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsConnection_ReadLastError(natsConnection *nc, char *buf, size_t n)
Read the last connection error into a user provided buffer.
natsConnection_Close
NATS_EXTERN void natsConnection_Close(natsConnection *nc)
Closes the connection.
natsConnection_Flush
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsConnection_Flush(natsConnection *nc)
Flushes the connection.
natsConnection_IsDraining
NATS_EXTERN bool natsConnection_IsDraining(natsConnection *nc)
Test if connection is draining.
natsConnection_Reconnect
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsConnection_Reconnect(natsConnection *nc)
Drops the current connection, reconnects including re-subscribing.
natsConnection_Status
NATS_EXTERN natsConnStatus natsConnection_Status(natsConnection *nc)
Returns the current state of the connection.
natsConnection_GetConnectedServerId
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsConnection_GetConnectedServerId(natsConnection *nc, char *buffer, size_t bufferSize)
Gets the server Id.
natsConnection_IsClosed
NATS_EXTERN bool natsConnection_IsClosed(natsConnection *nc)
Test if connection has been closed.
natsConnection_Drain
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsConnection_Drain(natsConnection *nc)
Drains the connection with default timeout.
natsConnection_IsReconnecting
NATS_EXTERN bool natsConnection_IsReconnecting(natsConnection *nc)
Test if connection is reconnecting.
natsConnection_GetStats
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsConnection_GetStats(natsConnection *nc, natsStatistics *stats)
Gets the connection statistics.
natsConnection_GetMaxPayload
NATS_EXTERN int64_t natsConnection_GetMaxPayload(natsConnection *nc)
Returns the maximum message payload.
natsConnection_Sign
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsConnection_Sign(natsConnection *nc, const unsigned char *message, int messageLen, unsigned char sig[64])
Signs any 'message' using the connection's user credentials.
natsConnection_GetName
NATS_EXTERN const char * natsConnection_GetName(natsConnection *nc)
Returns the name of this connection.
natsConnection_Connect
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsConnection_Connect(natsConnection **nc, natsOptions *options)
Connects to a NATS Server using the provided options.
natsConnection_GetClientID
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsConnection_GetClientID(natsConnection *nc, uint64_t *cid)
Gets the current client ID assigned by the server.
natsConnection_FlushTimeout
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsConnection_FlushTimeout(natsConnection *nc, int64_t timeout)
Flushes the connection with a given timeout.

NATS_EXTERN void natsConnection_ProcessDetachedEvent(natsConnection *nc)
Process a detach event when using external event loop.
natsConnection_ProcessCloseEvent
NATS_EXTERN void natsConnection_ProcessCloseEvent(natsSock *socket)
Process a socket close event when using external event loop.
natsConnection_GetLocalIPAndPort
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsConnection_GetLocalIPAndPort(natsConnection *nc, char **ip, int *port)
Returns the connection local IP and port.
natsConnection_GetRTT
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsConnection_GetRTT(natsConnection *nc, int64_t *rtt)
Returns the round trip time between this client and the server.
natsConnection_ProcessReadEvent
NATS_EXTERN void natsConnection_ProcessReadEvent(natsConnection *nc)
Process a read event when using external event loop.
natsConnection_GetLastError
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsConnection_GetLastError(natsConnection *nc, const char **lastError)
Gets the last connection error.
natsConnection_GetDiscoveredServers
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsConnection_GetDiscoveredServers(natsConnection *nc, char ***servers, int *count)
Returns the list of discovered server URLs.
natsConnection_HasHeaderSupport

Returns if the connection to current server supports headers.
natsConnection_ProcessWriteEvent
NATS_EXTERN void natsConnection_ProcessWriteEvent(natsConnection *nc)
Process a write event when using external event loop.
natsConnection_GetClientIP
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsConnection_GetClientIP(natsConnection *nc, char **ip)
Returns the client's IP address as reported by the server.
natsConnection_DrainTimeout

Drains the connection with given timeout.
natsConnection_GetServers
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsConnection_GetServers(natsConnection *nc, char ***servers, int *count)
Returns the list of server URLs known to this connection.
natsConnection_GetConnectedUrl

Gets the URL of the currently connected server.

Connects to a NATS Server using any of the URL from the given list.

Destroys the connection object.

Returns the name of the key that was retrieved.

Returns the value for this key.
kvEntry_Created

Returns the time (in UTC) the data was put in the bucket.
kvEntry_Revision
NATS_EXTERN uint64_t kvEntry_Revision(const kvEntry *e)
Returns the unique sequence for this value.
kvEntry_Delta
NATS_EXTERN uint64_t kvEntry_Delta(const kvEntry *e)
Returns the distance from the latest value.
kvEntry_Destroy

Destroys the KeyValue entry object.

Returns the value, as a string, for this key.

Returns the name of the bucket the data was loaded from.
kvEntry_Operation

Returns the type of operation of this value.
kvEntry_ValueLen

Initializes a KeyValue purge options structure.

Places the value (as a string) for the key into the store if and only if the key does not exist.

Returns a watcher for any updates to keys that match the keys argument.

Deletes a key by placing a purge marker and removing all revisions.

natsOptions_SetMessageBufferPadding
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsOptions_SetMessageBufferPadding(natsOptions *opts, int paddingSize)
Sets a custom padding when allocating buffer for incoming messages.
natsOptions_SetUserInfo
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsOptions_SetUserInfo(natsOptions *opts, const char *user, const char *password)
Sets the user name/password to use when not specified in the URL.
natsOptions_SetUserCredentialsFromMemory
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsOptions_SetUserCredentialsFromMemory(natsOptions *opts, const char *jwtAndSeedContent)
Sets JWT handler and handler to sign nonce that uses seed.
natsOptions_SetFlusherWaitMicros
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsOptions_SetFlusherWaitMicros(natsOptions *opts, int64_t flusherWaitUs)
Sets the maximum time the flusher thread waits to accumulate data.
natsOptions_SetUserCredentialsCallbacks
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsOptions_SetUserCredentialsCallbacks(natsOptions *opts, natsUserJWTHandler ujwtCB, void *ujwtClosure, natsSignatureHandler sigCB, void *sigClosure)
Sets the callbacks to fetch user JWT and sign server's nonce.
natsOptions_Create

Creates a natsOptions object.
natsOptions_SetIgnoreAuthErrorAbort
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsOptions_SetIgnoreAuthErrorAbort(natsOptions *opts, bool ignore)
Indicates if the connection should abort on authentication error or not.
natsOptions_SetNKeyFromSeed
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsOptions_SetNKeyFromSeed(natsOptions *opts, const char *pubKey, const char *seedFile)
Sets the NKey public key and its seed file.
natsOptions_AllowConcurrentTLSHandshakes
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsOptions_AllowConcurrentTLSHandshakes(natsOptions *opts)
Allows concurrent TLS handshakes.
natsOptions_SkipServerVerification
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsOptions_SkipServerVerification(natsOptions *opts, bool skip)
Switch server certificate verification.
natsOptions_UseOldRequestStyle

Switches the use of old style requests.
natsOptions_SetMaxPendingBytes
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsOptions_SetMaxPendingBytes(natsOptions *opts, int64_t maxPending)
Sets the maximum number of pending bytes per subscription.
natsOptions_IPResolutionOrder
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsOptions_IPResolutionOrder(natsOptions *opts, int order)
Dictates the order in which host name are resolved during connect.
natsOptions_SetIgnoreDiscoveredServers
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsOptions_SetIgnoreDiscoveredServers(natsOptions *opts, bool ignore)
Sets if the library should ignore or not discovered servers.
natsOptions_LoadCATrustedCertificatesPath
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsOptions_LoadCATrustedCertificatesPath(natsOptions *opts, const char *path)
Loads the trusted CA certificates from a directory.
natsOptions_SetSendAsap
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsOptions_SetSendAsap(natsOptions *opts, bool sendAsap)
Sets if Publish calls should send data right away.
natsOptions_SetReconnectJitter
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsOptions_SetReconnectJitter(natsOptions *opts, int64_t jitter, int64_t jitterTLS)
Set the upper bound of a random delay added to reconnect wait.

NATS_EXTERN void natsOptions_Destroy(natsOptions *opts)
Destroys a natsOptions object.
natsOptions_SetMaxPendingMsgs

Sets the maximum number of pending messages per subscription.
natsOptions_SetReconnectBufSize
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsOptions_SetReconnectBufSize(natsOptions *opts, int reconnectBufSize)
Sets the size of the backing buffer used during reconnect.
natsOptions_SetVerbose
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsOptions_SetVerbose(natsOptions *opts, bool verbose)
Sets the verbose mode.
natsOptions_SetSecure
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsOptions_SetSecure(natsOptions *opts, bool secure)
Sets the secure mode.
natsOptions_LoadCertificatesChainDynamic
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsOptions_LoadCertificatesChainDynamic(natsOptions *opts, const char *certsFileName, const char *keyFileName)
Loads the certificate chain and key from a file on every connection attempt.
natsOptions_SetNoRandomize
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsOptions_SetNoRandomize(natsOptions *opts, bool noRandomize)
Indicate if the servers list should be randomized.
natsOptions_SetWriteDeadline
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsOptions_SetWriteDeadline(natsOptions *opts, int64_t deadline)
Sets the write deadline.
natsOptions_SetMaxPingsOut
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsOptions_SetMaxPingsOut(natsOptions *opts, int maxPingsOut)
Sets the limit of outstanding PINGs without corresponding PONGs.

Sets the external event loop and associated callbacks.
natsOptions_SetMaxReconnect

Sets the maximum number of reconnect attempts.
natsOptions_SetDiscoveredServersCB
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus natsOptions_SetDiscoveredServersCB(natsOptions *opts, natsConnectionHandler discoveredServersCb, void *closure)
Sets the callback to be invoked when new servers are discovered.

Sets a custom inbox prefix.

Performs TLS handshake first.
natsOptions_SetServers

Set the list of servers to try to (re)connect to.

Sets the token to use when not specified in the URL.

natsOptions_SetCertificatesChain

js_PersistDefault
@ js_PersistDefault
Writes are immediately flushed, acknowledgement sent after message is stored.

js_PersistAsync
@ js_PersistAsync
Writes are flushed asynchronously, acknowledgement may be sent before message is stored.

@ js_DeliverLastPerSubject

js_DeliverLast
@ js_DeliverLast

Definition nats.h:386
js_DeliverAll
@ js_DeliverAll

@ js_LimitsPolicy

js_InterestPolicy
@ js_InterestPolicy
Specifies that when all known observables have acknowledged a message it can be removed.

js_WorkQueuePolicy
@ js_WorkQueuePolicy

Definition nats.h:347
js_DiscardNew
@ js_DiscardNew

Definition nats.h:357
js_DiscardOld
@ js_DiscardOld
Will remove older messages to return to the limits. This is the default.
Definition nats.h:356
js_ReplayInstant
@ js_ReplayInstant
Replays messages as fast as possible.
Definition nats.h:411
js_ReplayOriginal
@ js_ReplayOriginal
Maintains the same timing as the messages were received.
